arts.ttl: David Gray knows the Hibs sign that will spell real trouble as Easter Road boss adamant players are 'all in it together'
arts.sum: The Leith side can climb above rivals Hearts at the foot of the table if they hand Aberdeen a second successive defeat

arts.ttl: Graeme Shinnie backs Aberdeen to return to form but says he is 'wary' of Hibs threat
arts.sum: “I think if you reflect on games that they’ve had this season, they’ve been good and they’ve been five or 10 minutes away from being a lot higher up in the league,” he said. Jimmy Thelin’s side lost away from home to St Mirren in their first Scottish Premiership defeat of the season, bringing to an end their impressive 11-game unbeaten run in the league, which featured 10 wins.

arts.ttl: Jimmy Thelin reveals his Aberdeen debrief message and what he wasn't happy about against St Mirren
arts.sum: A defeat on Paisley saw the Reds lose in the Premiership for the first time since last March

arts.ttl: David Gray defends Hibs winger Elie Youan after pundit's 'unacceptable' verdict
arts.sum: My information went on very quickly as to what we wanted to do at the red card, especially for the next five minutes, to see what Dundee would do, to be honest, because of how well we started in the game. “I think anyone who knows Eli Youan knows he loves football,” the manager said.

arts.ttl: Fan anger and worsening results - is Gray's time at Hibs coming to an end?
arts.sum: Fans are angry and results are getting worse - will David Gray pay price with Hibernian bottom of the Scottish Premiership.

arts.ttl: Fan frustration at Rangers, can Gray survive at Hibs and who can stop Celtic?
arts.sum: The Rangers fans have had enough of the poor performances, the pressure is mounting on David Gray at HIbs while it appears Celtic are unstoppable in the Premiership.

arts.ttl: David Gray is right that Hibs have seen this movie before and there is one way to stop things turning X rated
arts.sum: The rock bottom side are facing up to a battle to avoid the drop

arts.ttl: What Jimmy Thelin told Aberdeen players after first league defeat – 'He sees the big picture'
arts.sum: Celtic moved three points clear of their rivals after the Dons slip against St Mirren
